assumptions: The Orthogonal Vectors problem is defined as: given two sets A, B ⊆ {0,1}^d of size n, decide if ∃a ∈ A, b ∈ B such that a · b = 0.
SETH implies that for every δ < 1, k-SAT cannot be solved in O(2^(δn)) time for any fixed k.
The imported paper arxiv_1312.6680 (Williams 2013) establishes faster APSP via circuit complexity, which is relevant background for the fine-grained complexity program.
The imported paper Abboud_2016_Simulating (Abboud, Hansen, Williams, Williams STOC 2016) extends SETH-based lower bounds from Edit Distance to other problems.
The imported paper arxiv_1502.01063 (Bringmann, Künnemann 2015) proves quadratic conditional lower bounds for LCS and DTW under SETH.
The imported paper Carmosino_2016_Nondeterministic (Carmosino et al. ITCS 2016) introduces NSETH and discusses its consequences.
The imported paper Bhattacharya_2020_Coarse (Bhattacharya, Nanongkai, Saranurak SODA 2020) discusses coarse-grained complexity for dynamic algorithms and mentions dynamic OV.
We work in the standard fine-grained complexity framework: conditional lower bounds are of the form 'Problem X cannot be solved in time T(n) unless SETH fails.'
SETH: For every δ < 1, there exists k such that k-SAT cannot be solved in O(2^(δn)) time. Equivalently, lim_{k→∞} sat-width(k-SAT) = 1.
OV (Orthogonal Vectors): Given two sets A, B ⊆ {0,1}^d of size n, decide if ∃a ∈ A, b ∈ B such that a · b = 0 (dot product over reals, i.e., no coordinate pair is (1,1)).
The OV conjecture: OV cannot be solved in O(n^(2-ε)) time for any ε > 0, for dimension d = n (or d = poly(n)).

The correct arXiv ID for Williams STOC 2009 'Finding, Minimizing, and Counting Weighted Subgraphs' is arXiv:0903.5300.

evidence.summary: Verified that arxiv_0811.3640 is a QCD paper (Bailey et al., B-meson form factor), not the Williams STOC 2009 paper. The correct arXiv ID for Williams STOC 2009 "Finding, Minimizing, and Counting Weighted Subgraphs" is arXiv:0903.5300. This paper is referenced in arxiv_1312.6680 but not imported as a standalone record. Only 4 relevant OV/SETH papers are imported; 3 core papers (Williams STOC 2009, Abboud-Williams STOC 2014, Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015) are missing.

runs.summary:
Literature audit for SETH-based OV lower bounds. The Williams STOC 2009 paper "Finding, Minimizing, and Counting Weighted Subgraphs" is NOT imported in the LiteratureDB. The arXiv ID 0811.3640 was incorrectly associated with this paper — it is actually a lattice QCD paper by Bailey et al. (form factor f_+(q^2) for B-meson semileptonic decay). The correct arXiv ID for the Williams STOC 2009 paper is arXiv:0903.5300 (Virginia Vassilevska Williams and Ryan Williams, STOC 2009, later published in SIAM J. Comput. 42(3):831–854, 2013 as [VW13]). This paper is referenced in the text of arxiv_1312.6680 (Williams 2013, "Faster all-pairs shortest paths via circuit complexity") but is not itself imported as a separate record.

Currently imported papers in LiteratureDB (18 total, only ~4 relevant to OV/SETH):
1. arxiv_1312.6680 — Ryan Williams, "Faster all-pairs shortest paths via circuit complexity" (2013) — relevant
2. arxiv_1502.01063 — Bringmann & Künnemann, "Quadratic Conditional Lower Bounds for String Problems and Dynamic Time Warping" (2015) — relevant
3. Bhattacharya_2020_Coarse — Bhattacharya, Nanongkai, Saranurak SODA 2020 — relevant
4. Carmosino_2016_Nondeterministic — Carmosino et al. ITCS 2016 — relevant
5. arxiv_0811.3640 — Bailey et al., QCD paper (WRONG — not relevant)
6. arxiv_1305.4561 — (needs verification)
7. arxiv_cs_0504066 — Schetinin et al., ML paper (not relevant)

Missing from import: Williams STOC 2009 (arXiv:0903.5300), Abboud-Williams STOC 2014, Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015. Only 4 relevant papers imported, falling short of the 5-core-paper success criterion. The SETH-OV lower bound theorem statements and quote-level provenance cannot be extracted because the foundational Williams 2009 paper is not imported.

evidence.summary: Multiple literature queries and direct reads of papers.jsonl and candidates.jsonl confirmed the absence of the target paper. The DOI 10.1145/2591796.2591810 was not found in any imported or queued record.

runs.summary: The assigned obligation to import Abboud-Williams STOC 2014 'Tight Hardness for Several NP-Complete Problems' (DOI 10.1145/2591796.2591810) could not be fulfilled because the paper is not present in the LiteratureDB. It was not imported during the initial literature ingestion, and it does not appear in the candidates queue either. Multiple searches by title, author names, and DOI confirmed its absence from both papers.jsonl and candidates.jsonl. The LiteratureDB contains 18 imported papers (including Williams 2013 on APSP, Bringmann-Künnemann 2015 on string problem lower bounds, and Abboud et al. 2016 on branching programs) but none match the target paper. Additionally, Williams STOC 2009 and Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015 are also absent, failing the success criterion of importing at least 5 core papers including these three.

evidence.summary: LiteratureDB query results confirm the paper is cited in other imported works (e.g., arxiv_1502.01063) but is not itself imported. The papers.jsonl and candidates.jsonl do not contain an entry for Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015.

runs.summary: The assigned obligation was to import the Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015 paper 'Edit Distance Cannot Be Computed in Strongly Subquadratic Time' via DOI search. I conducted extensive searches in the LiteratureDB (papers.jsonl, candidates.jsonl, query_answers.jsonl) for this paper. The paper is notков present in the imported papers list (papers.jsonl contains 18 papers, none by Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015). It is also not found in the candidates.jsonl (166 candidates, none matching the title/DOI). Literature queries for 'Backurs Indyk STOC 2015', 'Backurs Indyk Edit Distance', and 'arxiv 1412.0348' returned results referencing the paper (e.g., in the bibliography of arxiv_1502.01063 as '[6] Arturs Backurs and Piotr Indyk. Edit distance cannot be computed in strongly subquadratic time (unless SETH is false), 2014. arXiv 1412.0348.'), but did not result in the paper being imported or queued for import. The paper remains unimported. The obligation's success criteria (importing at least 5 core papers including this one) were not met. The paper is blocked by the lack of an automated import mechanism for this specific STOC 2015 paper via DOI in the current LiteratureDB state.
unresolved_blockers: The Backurs-Indyk STOC 2015 paper could not be imported via DOI search; it is not in the papers.jsonl or candidates.jsonl.
The obligation requires importing this specific paper, which remains unimported.

evidence.summary: LiteratureDB queries located the paper by authors and year, confirming the correct title and venue. However, the paper exists only as a citation in other imported papers' bibliographies, not as a fully imported standalone record with extracted text. The title mismatch ('Sparse Orthogonal Vectors' vs 'Completeness for first-order properties...') and lack of a direct import tool in this scope prevented full fulfillment.

runs.summary: The assigned obligation to import the Gao-Impagliazzo-Kolokolova-Williams SODA 2017 paper was partially investigated. LiteratureDB queries successfully located the paper, revealing that its actual title is 'Completeness for first-order properties on sparse structures with algorithmic applications' rather than 'Sparse Orthogonal Vectors'. The paper is present in the database only as a bibliographic citation within other imported works (e.g., Bhattacharya_2020_Coarse). A direct standalone import via DOI/arXiv with full-text extraction was not completed in this iteration, leaving the obligation blocked pending explicit import execution or text extraction.
